Test Scenario: NestJS server starts successfully on port 3000
Preconditions: PostgreSQL is running on localhost:5432; database 'budgetwise' exists; .env file contains DATABASE_URL and PORT=3000
Test Steps:
1. Open a terminal in the budgetwise-api/ directory
2. Run 'npm run start:dev'
3. Wait for the console output to stabilize
Expected Result: Server starts without errors. Console shows 'Nest application successfully started' or equivalent. No unhandled exceptions are logged. The process remains running and listening on port 3000.

Test Scenario: API responds under /api prefix
Test Steps:
1. Open Postman or use curl
2. Send GET http://localhost:3000/
3. Send GET http://localhost:3000/api
Expected Result: GET / returns 404 (no route mapped at root). GET /api returns either 404 or an empty response confirming the /api prefix is active. Neither request causes a server crash.

Test Scenario: Swagger documentation is accessible at /api/docs
Test Steps:
1. Open a browser
2. Navigate to http://localhost:3000/api/docs
Expected Result: Swagger UI loads successfully. The page title shows 'BudgetWise API'. API endpoint groups are visible in the documentation.

Test Scenario: CORS allows requests from Angular dev server origin
Test Steps:
1. Using curl or Postman send an OPTIONS preflight request to http://localhost:3000/api with the header 'Origin: http://localhost:4200'
2. Inspect the response headers
Expected Result: Response includes 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://localhost:4200'. 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' includes GET POST PATCH DELETE. 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials' is 'true'.

Test Scenario: ValidationPipe strips unknown properties (whitelist)
Preconditions: Server is running (TC-P1-001 passed); CRUD endpoints available (Ticket 03+)
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to http://localhost:3000/api/accounts with JSON body: {"name": "Test", "type": "CASH", "unknownField": "should be stripped"}
2. Observe the created record in the response or Prisma Studio
Expected Result: The record is created successfully. The 'unknownField' property is silently stripped and does not appear in the response or the database. No error is thrown for the extra property.

Test Scenario: ValidationPipe transforms payload types (transform)
Preconditions: Server is running (TC-P1-001 passed); CRUD endpoints available (Ticket 03+)
Test Steps:
1. Send a POST request to an endpoint that accepts typed fields (e.g. POST /api/budgets with month as a string '3' instead of number 3)
2. Observe the created record
Expected Result: The value is automatically transformed to the correct type (e.g. string '3' becomes number 3 in the DTO). The request succeeds without a type mismatch error.

Test Scenario: PostgreSQL database exists with all expected tables
Preconditions: PostgreSQL is running; migrations have been applied (npx prisma migrate dev)
Test Steps:
1. Open a terminal in the budgetwise-api/ directory
2. Connect to PostgreSQL using psql or pgAdmin
3. List all tables in the 'budgetwise' database
Expected Result: The database 'budgetwise' exists. Tables present include: Account Category Transaction Budget (plus Prisma migration tracking table _prisma_migrations). All tables match the schema models.

Test Scenario: Account model schema is correct
Test Steps:
1. Open Prisma Studio (npx prisma studio) or inspect the Account table schema via psql/pgAdmin
2. Verify each column's name and type
Expected Result: Account table has columns: id (UUID primary key auto-generated) name (String not null) type (AccountType enum: CASH/BANK/EWALLET) balance (Decimal(12 2) default 0) createdAt (DateTime default now()) updatedAt (DateTime auto-updated). A relation to Transaction exists (one-to-many).

Test Scenario: Category model schema is correct
Test Steps:
1. Open Prisma Studio or inspect the Category table schema
2. Verify each column's name and type
3. Verify the unique constraint on 'name'
Expected Result: Category table has columns: id (UUID primary key) name (String unique not null) icon (String nullable) createdAt (DateTime default now()). Relations exist to Transaction (one-to-many) and Budget (one-to-many). The name column has a unique index.

Test Scenario: Transaction model schema is correct
Test Steps:
1. Open Prisma Studio or inspect the Transaction table schema
2. Verify each column's name and type
3. Verify foreign key relationships and their delete behaviors
Expected Result: Transaction table has columns: id (UUID primary key) type (TransactionType enum: INCOME/EXPENSE) amount (Decimal(12 2) not null) description (String nullable) date (DateTime default now()) accountId (FK to Account onDelete Cascade) categoryId (FK to Category onDelete Restrict) createdAt (DateTime) updatedAt (DateTime auto-updated).

Test Scenario: Budget model schema is correct
Test Steps:
1. Open Prisma Studio or inspect the Budget table schema
2. Verify each column's name and type
3. Verify the composite unique constraint on [categoryId month year]
Expected Result: Budget table has columns: id (UUID primary key) amount (Decimal(12 2) not null) month (Int not null) year (Int not null) categoryId (FK to Category onDelete Cascade) createdAt (DateTime) updatedAt (DateTime auto-updated). A composite unique constraint exists on (categoryId month year).

Test Scenario: Prisma Studio opens and shows empty tables
Preconditions: Database exists with migrations applied (TC-P1-007 passed); no seed data has been run yet
Test Steps:
1. Open a terminal in the budgetwise-api/ directory
2. Run 'npx prisma studio'
3. Wait for the browser to open
4. Click through each model: Account Category Transaction Budget
Expected Result: Prisma Studio opens in the browser (typically at http://localhost:5555). All models are listed in the sidebar. Clicking each model shows an empty table with the correct column headers. No records exist yet.

Test Scenario: .env file is excluded from version control
Preconditions: Git repository is initialized; .gitignore exists in budgetwise-api/
Test Steps:
1. Open budgetwise-api/.gitignore in a text editor
2. Verify '.env' is listed
3. Run 'git status' in the budgetwise-api/ directory
Expected Result: The .gitignore file contains a '.env' entry. Running 'git status' does not show .env as a tracked or untracked file. The .env file exists locally but is excluded from the repository.

Test Scenario: PrismaModule is globally available (@Global decorator)
Test Steps:
1. Open src/prisma/prisma.module.ts and verify the @Global() decorator is present
2. Open src/app.module.ts and verify PrismaModule is in the imports array
3. Confirm the server starts without dependency injection errors
Expected Result: prisma.module.ts has the @Global() decorator on the module class. PrismaModule is imported in app.module.ts. The server starts without 'Nest could not find PrismaService' injection errors confirming PrismaService is available globally.

Test Scenario: AccountType enum has correct values
Test Steps:
1. Inspect the schema.prisma file or query the PostgreSQL enum type
2. Verify the AccountType enum values
Expected Result: AccountType enum contains exactly three values: CASH BANK EWALLET. No other values exist.

Test Scenario: TransactionType enum has correct values
Test Steps:
1. Inspect the schema.prisma file or query the PostgreSQL enum type
2. Verify the TransactionType enum values
Expected Result: TransactionType enum contains exactly two values: INCOME EXPENSE. No other values exist.

Test Scenario: Seed command executes without errors
Preconditions: Ticket 01 is complete; database exists with empty tables
Test Steps:
1. Open a terminal in the budgetwise-api/ directory
2. Run 'npx prisma db seed'
3. Observe the console output
Expected Result: The command completes without errors. Console output includes 'Seed data loaded successfully.' No exceptions or warnings are printed.

Test Scenario: 11 categories are created with correct names
Test Steps:
1. Open Prisma Studio (npx prisma studio)
2. Click on the Category model
3. Count the total number of records
4. Verify each category name
Expected Result: Exactly 11 categories exist. Names are: Food & Dining / Transport / Bills & Utilities / Shopping / Entertainment / Health / Education / Savings / Salary / Freelance / Other. All names match exactly including ampersands and spacing.

Test Scenario: All categories have correct emoji icons
Test Steps:
1. In Prisma Studio view the Category model
2. Check the 'icon' column for each category
Expected Result: Each category has the correct emoji: Food & Dining = hamburger / Transport = car / Bills & Utilities = lightbulb / Shopping = shopping bags / Entertainment = game controller / Health = pill / Education = books / Savings = money bag / Salary = briefcase / Freelance = laptop / Other = pushpin. No icon field is null.

Test Scenario: All categories have UUID primary keys
Test Steps:
1. In Prisma Studio view the Category model
2. Inspect the 'id' column for each record
Expected Result: Every category record has a valid UUID (format: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx) as its id. All 11 ids are unique.

Test Scenario: 3 default accounts are created with correct names and types
Test Steps:
1. In Prisma Studio click on the Account model
2. Count the total number of records
3. Verify each account's name and type
Expected Result: Exactly 3 accounts exist. Cash with type CASH / Bank Account with type BANK / E-Wallet with type EWALLET.

Test Scenario: All default accounts have balance of 0
Test Steps:
1. In Prisma Studio view the Account model
2. Check the 'balance' column for each account
Expected Result: All 3 accounts have a balance of 0.00. No account has a non-zero starting balance.

Test Scenario: All accounts have UUID primary keys and timestamps
Test Steps:
1. In Prisma Studio view the Account model
2. Inspect the 'id' and 'createdAt' and 'updatedAt' columns
Expected Result: Each account has a valid UUID as id. createdAt and updatedAt are both populated with valid datetime values. All 3 ids are unique.

Test Scenario: Seed is idempotent — running twice does not create duplicates
Preconditions: Seed has been run once (TC-P1-017 passed)
Test Steps:
1. Run 'npx prisma db seed' a second time
2. Observe the console output
3. Open Prisma Studio and count records in Category and Account models
Expected Result: The command completes without errors. Console shows 'Seed data loaded successfully.' Category count is still exactly 11. Account count is still exactly 3. No duplicate records were created.

Test Scenario: Transaction and Budget tables remain empty after seeding
Test Steps:
1. In Prisma Studio click on the Transaction model
2. Click on the Budget model
3. Verify record counts
Expected Result: Transaction table has 0 records. Budget table has 0 records. The seed script only creates categories and accounts.
